title: AIATSIS Map
caption: Map of Indigenous Australia showing the hundreds of distinct nations and language groups.
prompt: How does this map prove the diversity of First Nations groups?

title: Acknowledging Country
caption: A text describing the protocols and reasons for Acknowledging Country as a sign of respect.
prompt: Why is this protocol a Cultural necessity today?

title: Totems and Culture
caption: Information on how totems link people to specific animals and parts of the environment.
prompt: How do totems preserve both culture and nature?

title: Makasar Traders
caption: An artwork depicting Macassan fishermen interacting with Aboriginal people in Northern Australia.
prompt: What does this tell us about international Social connections?

title: Monsoon Trade
caption: Details on how the monsoon winds dictated the timing of the trepang trade.
prompt: How did the Environment dictate the timing of ancient trade?

title: Axe Heads Route
caption: A report on stone axe heads being traded over 1000km from their source quarry.
prompt: What does the 1000km distance suggest about Social networks?

title: Symbolism of the Flag
caption: A description of the Torres Strait Islander flag and what the blue, green, and black represent.
prompt: How does the flag represent Cultural identity?

title: Crocodile Mask
caption: A traditional mask made from turtle shell representing an ancestor totem.
prompt: What is the Cultural significance of the crocodile totem here?

title: Traditional Canoe
caption: A photo of a traditional Saibai Island canoe used for long-distance seafaring.
prompt: How did this technology allow for Environmental navigation?
